headline: Is AEP’s OVEC Restructuring And Data-Center Load Surge Altering The Investment Case For American Electric Power Company (AEP)?
summary: In April 2026, the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission approved, and on June 1 American Electric Power Company completed, a multi-part transaction shifting Ohio Power Company’s OVEC power entitlement to AEP Generation Resources and its OVEC equity stake to the parent company. This restructuring concentrates OVEC-related ownership and contracts higher in AEP’s corporate structure, which could affect how the company manages legacy generation exposure, regulatory oversight, and future capital...

headline: How The Story Is Shifting For American Electric Power Company (AEP) On Data Center Growth And Risk
summary: The modeled fair value for American Electric Power Company has shifted slightly, moving from US$144.29 to US$144.52, which reflects only a very small adjustment to the price target. This kind of fine tuning lines up with recent Street research that focuses less on big valuation swings and more on how data center demand, rising capital plans, and regulatory or political constraints might shape the story from here. As you read on, you will see how these moving pieces feed into the evolving...
